title: Email tracking against company rather than person

posts.author: DannyEvans (Danny Evans)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!

Is it possible to manually track email updates from Outlook against a company rather than an individual?

The CRM I am building is designed so that a company is the main record rather than the individuals who work for that company, so I need a solution where the data still exists for that company once an individual moves on.

The Outlook integration works so well against individuals, and I’m happy to use a manual workaround if it comes to it, I just can’t see how to do it

Any help you can offer will be hugely appreciated.

posts.author: abonsey (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml: @DannyEvans - Did you ever get a solution in place? I asked the same question yesterday

posts.author: DannyEvans (Danny Evans)
posts.cookedHtml: Nope, I’m back here again a year on still trying to figure it out

posts.author: abonsey (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml:
@DannyEvans We too had that need… and still do!!

I got our own app built to add email update to the company to ensure when looking at the company you could see all correspondence from anybody within the company.
It enabled our CSRs to quickly see at a glance when the company was last contacted in any way.

Happy to discuss. Send me a message.

posts.author: YurriMurray (YurriMurray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Danny Evans:

Hi!

The CRM I am building is designed so that a company is the main record rather than the individuals who work for that company, so I need a solution where the data still exists for that company once an individual moves on.

The Outlook integration works so well against individuals, and I’m happy to use a manual workaround if it comes to it, I just can’t see how to do it agencia de digital marketing

Any help you can offer will be hugely appreciated.

Tag emails with company names, encourage company email use, set up shared mailboxes, or manually enter critical updates into the CRM.

posts.author: phawley (Peter Hawley)
posts.cookedHtml: You might try extracting the domain name of the sender and associating that with the company.
